data.title: Prosperity Bancshares will acquire Stellar Bancorp for $2.002 billion, creating the second largest Texas bank.
data.description: Prosperity Bancshares, Inc. (PB) and Stellar Bancorp, Inc. (STEL) have seen their stock prices move in different directions following the announcement of their respective earnings reports and a merger agreement. Under the deal, Prosperity will exchange 0.3803 of its common stock shares along with $11.36 in cash for each share of Stellar common stock. The agreement values the transaction at around $2.002 billion, calculated based on Prosperity's closing price of $72.90 on January 27, 2026. Both companies' Boards of Directors have unanimously approved the merger, which is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending regulatory clearances and standard closing conditions. data.title: Natural gas up 30%, silver up 13% to $116 amidst market movements, Henry Hub contract trades above $6, gold rose 2.2%, dollar weak.
data.description: U.S. natural gas prices surged more than 30% due to Winter Storm Fern, causing the largest weekly percentage gain for Nymex front-month contracts. Henry Hub front-month contract surpassed $6 per million British thermal units from $3 a week ago, marking a 125% increase. Gold rose 2.2% to $5,100, while silver surged 13% to $116 per ounce, with a rolling 12-month gain of 266%, the highest since 1980. The dollar weakened and the Japanese yen strengthened; there were speculations of currency intervention involving the Federal Reserve Bank of New York. U.S. equities also saw an upward trend. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

MUSALEM SAYS HE EXPECTS ECONOMY TO CONTINUE GROWING ABOVE TREND, BOOSTED BY CREDIT CONDITIONS AND FISCAL POLICY

data.title: Gold hits record high at $5,300/oz, up 22% this month. Silver at $114/oz, surging 59% year-to-date. Memory stocks soar after Seagate's guidance raise. Wall Street sees modest gains. Fed likely to keep interest rates unchanged at 3.50%–3.75%.
data.description: On Wednesday, Wall Street saw moderate gains with gold reaching a record high above $5,300 an ounce before the Federal Open Market Committee meeting. Gold has surged over 22% this month, its best rise since January 1980. Silver also climbed nearly 3% to $114 per ounce, aiming for its highest month on record after a 59% rise year-to-date. US equities started strong, with the S&P 500 touching 7,000 briefly before dropping 30 points during midday trading. The Nasdaq 100 rose 0.4%, surpassing 26,000 towards the October record of 26,182. Chipmakers like Seagate Technology Inc. beat expectations, raising full-year guidance due to global supply issues and high AI demand, sending its shares up 20%. Rivals Sandisk Corp. and Western Digital Corp. rose 7.3% and 9% respectively, whereas Micron Technology Inc. gained over 5%. The Federal Reserve is expected to maintain interest rates at 3.50%–3.75%, hinting at no urgency to adjust borrowing costs. Chair Jerome Powell's upcoming press conference is under scrutiny following a recent criminal investigation into the Fed's building renovation project.

data.title: Fed keeps federal funds rate at 3.5%–3.75% after 3 cuts, stating solid economic pace but elevated inflation.
data.description: The Federal Reserve decided to maintain the federal funds rate at 3.5%–3.75% in their January 2026 statement, halting the easing trend following three rate decreases last year, which dropped borrowing expenses to their lowest level since 2022. The Federal Open Market Committee upgraded its characterization of economic growth to a "solid pace," a change from the previous description of "moderate pace." The statement noted that the unemployment rate has exhibited signs of stabilization, a shift from earlier indications that it had slightly increased. The Federal Reserve acknowledged that inflation levels are still "somewhat elevated." Fed Chair Jerome Powell was also mentioned in the news release.

data.title: Bitcoin struggles below $84,000 due to ETF outflows, with $1.43 billion liquidated in 24 hours. Ethereum dips to $2,715.
data.description: Bitcoin is currently struggling to maintain a value of $84,000, with heavy outflows from a U.S. spot ETF indicating institutional hesitancy and leading to a new round of liquidations. The current prices for cryptocurrencies are as follows: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) at $83,721, Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) at $2,715, Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) at $117.96, XRP (CRYPTO: XRP) at $1.77, Dogecoin (CRYPTO: DOGE) at $0.1178, and Shiba Inu (CRYPTO: SHIB) at $0.05. Within the past 24 hours, a total of 203,945 traders were liquidated, resulting in losses totaling $1.43 billion according to Coinglass data. Prominent losers in the last day include River, Tether Gold, and PAX Gold. To read more about the reasons behind Bitmine Immersion Stock's decline on Friday and insights from Raoul Pal regarding Bitcoin's unchanged thesis despite recent liquidation events, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Ripple's CTO doubts $50-$100 XRP, price drops 12% to $1.75. Backlash from holders. Schwartz stands by skepticism.
data.description: Ripple's Chief Technology Officer, David Schwartz, has caused controversy among XRP holders by expressing skepticism about the token's potential to reach $50 to $100. XRP's value plummeted by 12% to $1.75 following his comments. Schwartz admitted his past price forecasts were inaccurate, recalling selling XRP at $0.10 when he thought it was overvalued. XRP holders criticized Schwartz for undermining confidence in the token's future. Some questioned whether Ripple's original vision for XRP has shifted. This backlash came in response to Schwartz's dismissal of the $50-100 price predictions.

data.title: Brown Plus ranked among top 2025 P&C insurance audit firms in the U.S. by AM Best. Leading in the industry.
data.description: In Frederick, Maryland on January 30, 2026, Brown Plus, a top accounting and advisory firm, secured a notable achievement by being recognized as one of the leading audit firms catering to property and casualty (P&C) insurance companies in the U.S. This accolade was bestowed upon them by AM Best, as they made it onto the prestigious Top Audit and Actuarial Firms - 2025 list. This recognition solidifies Brown Plus's position in the industry and affirms their standing among the largest firms operating in this sector.

data.title: SPAC supported by Kraken raises $345M in expanded Nasdaq debut, creating public vehicle linked to US crypto exchange.
data.description: The special-purpose acquisition company has not identified a specific takeover target yet, but the listing establishes a fresh publicly traded entity linked to the American cryptocurrency exchange.

data.title: Cincinnati Financial Corp. raises quarterly cash dividend to 94 cents per share, an 8% increase from previous 87 cents.
data.description: In Cincinnati on January 30, 2026, the Cincinnati Financial Corporation (Nasdaq: CINF) disclosed in a press release that during the latest board meeting, a regular quarterly cash dividend of 94 cents per share was approved. This marks an 8% increase from the prior dividend of 87 cents per share.

data.title: CREFC President congratulates Kevin Warsh on his nomination as Federal Reserve Chair in New York press release.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, the CRE Finance Council (CREFC) released a statement through Lisa Pendergast, President and CEO, regarding Kevin Warsh's nomination as Chair of the Federal Reserve. Pendergast praised Warsh for his nomination and offered congratulations on behalf of CREFC. The specific details of the statement were not provided, but it highlighted support for Warsh in his potential role at the Federal Reserve.

data.title: BTC futures may lead to liquidation rally to $90K as short positions increase near $81K.
data.description: Short positions on Bitcoin rose significantly as the price of BTC plummeted to approximately $81,000, setting the stage for possible liquidations that could fuel a strong rally pushing the price back up to over $90,000.

data.title: Investors in Coupang, Inc. (NYSE: CPNG) are alerted about potential lawsuit over major account breach. Deadline: February 17, 2026. Securities bought between May 7, 2025, and December 16 are being scrutinized.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, it was reported that Hagens Berman, a national shareholder rights firm, is reminding investors of a lead plaintiff deadline of February 17, 2026, for Coupang, Inc. (NYSE: CPNG). This reminder is specifically for investors who bought Coupang securities (CPNG) between May 7, 2025, and December 16. Those eligible for participation need to act before the given deadline to be considered for involvement in the case.

data.title: High-net-worth traders adopt portfolio-level trading for sustainable performance. Exposing risks across accounts is vital for professional execution.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, in Scottsdale, Arizona, it was reported that high-net-worth traders are realizing the importance of diversification in trading to achieve sustainable performance. They understand that concentrating risk in a single account is not effective. Professional traders are now focusing on portfolio-level trading for better execution. This approach allows for better exposure management and risk mitigation.
